findings.message: Duplicate data-access layer: importing from item_service instead of the existing repository module. Both modules provide equivalent functions (fetch_all_items vs list_items, add_item vs create_item), creating unnecessary duplication and maintenance burden.
findings.suggestion: Use the existing repository module (repository.list_items and repository.create_item) which already encapsulates data-access logic. Remove item_service imports and consolidate on a single data layer.

findings.message: Layering violation: route handler directly calls session.commit() and session.refresh(). This puts transaction management into the request handler, violating separation of concerns. The data layer should encapsulate these operations completely.
findings.suggestion: Move commit() and refresh() into item_service.add_item() or use repository.create_item() which already handles these operations (see repository.py:14-15). The route should only orchestrate, not manage transactions.
